title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Luca Langoni's return and Luca's decisive goal highlighted Revolution's win, benefitting from strategic pressing in midfield.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner made crucial saves, responding to criticism from a recent performance and boosting the team’s confidence.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted the season 51 finale of 'Saturday Night Live' on May 16, joined by musical guest Paul McCartney.
Ferrell's cold open featured him as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost with James Austin Johnson playing Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' and 'Band on the Run' during the SNL episode.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur July 11-16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries for the U.S. 250th anniversary.
Previously, Sail Boston events were held in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, drawing millions of spectators.
The 1992 event saw protests from Black Americans citing tall ships as reminders of the slave trade, which continued into 2000.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returned to Patriots minicamp, bringing energy that improved the building's vibe, despite his off-field challenges.
New England Patriots are expected to trade for wide receiver A.J. Brown, enhancing their 2026 roster.
Safety Kevin Byard praised Vrabel's energy and consistency, seeing no change from his coaching days in Tennessee.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a go-ahead two-run home run in the eighth inning to lift the Red Sox over the Braves 3-2.
Payton Tolle pitched eight innings for the Red Sox, allowing two runs and four hits, with three strikeouts.
Aroldis Chapman secured the win with a save, despite a tense ninth inning with bases loaded against the Braves.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list by Red Sox due to a sports hernia.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A to replace Story on the Red Sox roster.
Red Sox consider surgery for Story, which could require a 6-10 week recovery.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: David Peloski, 74, died when his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
13 students from Monadnock Regional Middle High School were on the bus; no serious injuries were reported.
The crash on Route 12 remains under investigation by New Hampshire State Police and local authorities.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Braves' Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single to give Atlanta a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Boston's record fell to 18-26 after leaving seven men on base and batting 1-for-3 in scoring positions.
Red Sox starter Connelly Early allowed two runs over five innings with six strikeouts and no walks.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A reunion between Stefon Diggs and the Patriots is unlikely due to the imminent A.J. Brown trade, per Albert Breer.
Diggs, interested in returning to New England, is hindered by potential salary cap issues if the Brown trade occurs.
Despite Diggs's strong performance last season, the Patriots prioritize cap space for Brown and other new acquisitions.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester, according to Boston police.
Police describe LaGrant-Gomes as a Black male, 5'8", with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Anyone with information should contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit tips via CrimeStoppers.
